entries.notes: M1 golden sample created and browser-checked with Playwright desktop/mobile canvas pixel checks. Still mapped to the broader Oscillations and Waves node until an exact graph-node data change is reviewed. Riku triage 2026-07-12: published.

M2 page 02. Golden-sample template reused (structure/CSS/canvas scaffold). Streamlined per Riku feedback: static render-on-demand canvas (no auto-flow), a single control (N = wave count, 1->8), and only two curves on the canvas (faint dashed target square + bright synth). The 'sum of specific sines' logic moved from an on-canvas component overlay to an explicit expanding-sum formula (sin + 1/3 sin3 + 1/5 sin5 + ...). Browser-verified desktop/mobile: synth math independently checked (converges to +/-1 square with ~9% Gibbs overshoot that persists; N=1 = 4/pi fundamental), single control + 44px touch target confirmed, static render paints without rAF/visible-tab dependency, no console errors. Still mapped to the broader Fourier Analysis node. Pending Riku five-minute test.

M3 page 03. Opus-designed / Sonnet-built from the golden-sample template. Two resize-robust canvases: draggable-free 2nd-order poles in the s-plane (left) driving the unit-step response (right), two controls (zeta 0-1.2, wn 1-3). Math independently cross-checked (throwaway RK4 vs closed forms, max dev ~1e-12 across all four regimes; %OS zeta=0.3->37.2%, 0.5->16.3%, 0.707->4.3%; phi=arccos to stay clean at zeta=0; wn-invariance of the normalized curve confirmed by pixel comparison; settling-time 4/(zeta*wn) kept as approx, not drawn). Riku triage 2026-07-12: published after general usability testing; the line-by-line formula-vs-textbook check this page's own notes flagged as a hard blocker for Riku's control-theory track was NOT completed before publishing -- accepted as a known, tracked risk (see depth/files/BACKLOG.md), not silently dropped.

M3 page 04 (transformer). Opus-designed / Sonnet-built from the golden-sample template. Teaches ONE self-attention head over a fixed 7-token sentence: tap a query token, see its softmax attention (7x7 heatmap + stacked weight bar + plain-language readout), default selection 'it' shows it->cat=0.83 on load. Honest scope stated on-page and in notes: Q/K/V are hand-picked ILLUSTRATIVE constants (not learned), v=k simplification, single head, no positional encoding / feed-forward / stacking / training. Math live in JS (stable softmax over score=q.k/sqrt(d), d=4): verified it->cat 0.83, hid/scared->cat 0.72, every row sums to 1; sqrt(d) demo (0.83 vs 0.99 without) shipped; optional temperature slider keeps rows summing to 1. Mapped to the broad Deep Learning node. Pending Riku five-minute test.

entries.content_focus: 改變波包寬度，親手看見波函數如何決定重複位置測量的分布。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate. Uses the broader Quantum Mechanics field because no exact canonical wavefunction node exists; exact graph mapping requires a separate data/layout migration.

entries.content_focus: 改變兩路徑相對相位，親手看見疊加振幅可以相長或相消。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate. Mapped to Wave-Particle Duality as the closest existing canonical context; the page itself teaches only superposition.

entries.content_focus: 壓窄位置分布，親手看見最小不確定性高斯態的動量分布同步展寬。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate. Existing graph lacks an exact uncertainty-principle node; this broader mapping is intentionally low-confidence and must not be mistaken for exact identity.

entries.content_focus: 加厚有限矩形勢壘，親手看見穿透率快速下降但不被一刀切成零。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate. Semiconductor is the closest existing canonical application context; exact tunneling identity requires a later graph migration.

entries.content_focus: 旋轉 Stern–Gerlach 測量方向，親手看見比例連續改變但單次輸出保持兩種。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate. Spectroscopy is a related existing canonical node, not an exact synonym for spin; mapping stays low-confidence.

entries.content_focus: 逐對測量 singlet，親手看見每邊個別隨機而同軸配對結果始終相反。
entries.notes: First-batch candidate with an exact canonical graph mapping. The interaction deliberately stops before Bell-inequality claims.
